mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 4.0 percent indicates labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of months where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during expansionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand that sustains corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series shows quarter-over-quarter deceleration, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production above 102 index points reflects peak manufacturing capacity utilization that supports short-term equity momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses to negative returns during late-cycle expansions, or 5-day forward returns show no significant difference from baseline when INDPRO exceeds 102.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent high federal funds rates and elevated consumer price indices create a stagflationary environment that forces aggressive discount rate adjustments, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect disappears when CPIAUCSL growth decelerates below 3% annually, or 20-day returns remain positive in >50% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained nonfarm payroll employment above 158 million persons guarantees robust household income growth that fuels equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where PAYEMS exceeds 158 million, or rolling Rank IC drops below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.2 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and related consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months where HOUST drops below 1200, or the threshold effect is statistically insignificant in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 102 index points during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity restoration that boosts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns when macro volatility (VIX > 25) coincides with high INDPRO, or 20-day returns are negative in >50% of triggered instances during recovery phases.
